expected_trades_per_day_basis: Reserved6 channel emits ~180s extreme-move alerts; with high-liquidity gate (top quartile) and long-only side, estimated ~35-40 triggers per 30-day window based on R6's 35 entry archetypes baseline density and ~50% high-liq pass rate, yielding ~1.2/day.

best_in: BTC trend-up days with broad alt participation
periods of negative funding on majors (short squeeze fuel)
post-crash V-recovery sessions where R6 fires on the rebound leg
worst_in: sustained BTC downtrend where R6 'extreme moves' are crash continuations not reversals
low-vol consolidation weeks (R6 barely fires, surviving fires are noise)
high-funding-positive regimes (long-side edge compressed by funding cost)
regime_sensitivity.explanation: R6 long with only a liquidity gate has no directional micro-filter, so it inherits raw channel bias. Reserved6 historically skews short-favorable (180s 8-15% moves are usually crashes), so a long-only variant will be regime-fragile and only shine when the broader tape supports mean-reversion or continuation-up.

live_readiness_caveats: High-liquidity bucket still includes mid-caps with 5-15bps slippage on market entry — assume 0.20% round-trip cost floor, not the backtest's frictionless fill.

Devil's warning is load-bearing: with only liquidity_bucket=high as the novel_dim, lift vs plain R6/long baseline must exceed +0.05 or this is a silent-fallthrough duplicate (R1 bug echo) — kill on first weekly review if delta is inside noise.
R6 alerts cluster in time (multiple symbols firing within minutes during market-wide moves) — expect concurrent positions and size each entry at 5% not 10% to keep aggregate risk bounded.

monitoring_metrics.rationale: This archetype's ENTIRE thesis is that high-liquidity gating adds edge over raw R6/long. If lift collapses to baseline, the filter is cosmetic and the strategy has no reason to exist separately.
Under-firing means liquidity bucket definition drifted (universe changed); over-firing means the filter is too loose and you're trading raw R6 noise.
R6 long is structurally fighting channel bias; once the median goes red the asymmetric TP/SL trap from E126 paper repeats — left tail will drain account before mean recovers.
Win rate on R6 long is expected ~55-60%; a 6-loss streak is a 1-in-100 event and signals regime has flipped against the long bias.

monitoring_metrics.metric: fill_rate on high-liq symbols at signal+1s
monitoring_metrics.rationale: R6 fires on already-extreme 180s moves; if fills at +1s are missing, you're chasing and the backtest entry price is fiction.

stop_trading_triggers.rationale: Confirms both absolute drawdown and the specific filter-edge thesis have failed simultaneously — no path to recovery without re-derivation.

stop_trading_triggers.condition: Cumulative paper-shadow PnL < -8% on $1000 over any 14-day window
stop_trading_triggers.rationale: Mirrors the E126 compounding trap: asymmetric TP/SL with left-tail blindness drained -13.5% in R2; -8% is the early-exit line before unrecoverable damage.

stop_trading_triggers.condition: liquidity_bucket=high universe shrinks <30 symbols (Binance delisting wave or volume collapse)
stop_trading_triggers.rationale: The filter's statistical basis disappears; remaining symbols are not the same population the archetype was scored on.

expected_max_dd_basis: Anchored to E126's observed -13.5% compounding loss as the worst-case sibling (asymmetric TP/SL, long-side, similar trigger density); liquidity gate should reduce slip-driven tail by ~30%, projecting ~9% max DD over a 30-day paper window.
compare_to_existing: E051 pc_high_liquidity_long (best=+0.347, lift=+0.427, trig=2157) — same filter philosophy (liquidity-only gate, long side) but on pricechange channel where long bias is supported; E211 is the R6-channel sibling and should be triangulated against E051 weekly. If E211 lift sits >0.10 below E051's, the R6 channel is the bottleneck not the filter.
